'''Lafra''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Wyl sector/Legends|Wyl sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] with a variety of terrains. It was the home world of the [[Lafrarian]]s, gray-skinned, avian [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] who were once flyers but lost this ability over eons of evolutionary change. They built their homes in high, inaccessible locations on the planet (such as treetops or high mountain sides). Nearly everyone on the world owned a flying conveyance, with few land or [[Water/Legends|water]] [[Transport/Legends|transports]] seeing use.

An unfortunate incident involving an improperly maintained [[CSPL-12 projectile launcher/Legends|CSPL-12]] [[Projectile launcher/Legends|projectile launcher]] resulted in the untimely death of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Azarin]] on this world.<ref name="ISB">''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''</ref>

The planet's [[Smashball/Legends|smashball]] team played against that of [[Gathus/Legends|Gathus]].

==Behind the scenes==
The PC game [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] mistakenly places the Orus sector and, consequently, Lafra, in the Inner Rim, also erroneously stating that this sector is near [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion]]'' places Lafra in the [[Wyl sector/Legends|Wyl sector]], and thus, the Outer Rim.
